title: lupinus excubitus subsp. austromontanus
intro: Lupinus excubitus subsp. austromontanus, also known as the Santa Catalina Island Bush Lupine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family, native to the Santa Catalina Island in California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Lupinus excubitus subsp. austromontanus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Lupinus excubitus subsp. austromontanus is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
